DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DEBBRAH RUFFIN MOTION - NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDERS AND SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 2033 280 AND FOR AN ORDER THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL VERIFY BY DECLARATION COMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT 3 510. THE REQUEST THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL PREPARE A DECLARATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS THIS WAS NOT THE SUBJECT OF ANY FORMAL DISCOVERY REQUEST. AS FOR THE MOTION REGARDING THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SINCE TIMELY RESPONSES WERE MADE THAT CONTAINED OBJECTIONS, THE OBJECTIONS ARE PRESERVED EVEN WITHOUT A VERIFICATION. (SEE BROWN & WEIL, 8:1360; 8:1364.) SINCE THE VERIFICATION WAS NOT PROVIDED UNTIL AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED, SANCTIONS ARE WARRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1560 AS RUFFIN DID NOT PREVAIL ON ALL ISSUES. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT RKANE1089@AOL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROBERT KANE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR SEP-08-2020 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON DEC-14-2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. LAURA SEIDL, ESQ. AND NICOMEDES HERRERA, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. KATHRYN KLAUS, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. DANIEL JACOBS, COURT CERTIFIED SPANISH INTERPRETER WITH OATH ON FILE, PRESENT AND ASSISTED DEFENDANT RAFAEL GUZMAN. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE TRIAL OVER PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION. THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED TO 12/14/20 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY REMAINS CLOSED THAT INCLUDES ANY 2034 DISCOVERY WITH THE EXCEPTION OF DEMURRER AND MOTIONS TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 302. A VOLUNTARY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS ASSIGNED TO JUDGE ANGELA BRADSTREET. ALL PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO APPEAR ON 12/14/20. NOTICE IS WAIVED. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders And Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2033 280 And For An Order That Defense Counsel Verify By Declaration Compliance With California Rules Of Professional Conduct 3 510
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laura Seidl, Esq. 510-422-4700 for Plaintiff.
Thomas Yen, Esq. 650-592-5400 of Coddington HIcks & Danforth for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem and both sides submit to the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEBBRAH RUFFIN MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders And Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2033 280 And For An Order That Defense Counsel Verify By Declaration Compliance With California Rules Of Professional Conduct 3 510.

The request that defense counsel prepare a declaration is denied without prejudice as this was not the subject of any formal discovery request. As for the Motion regarding the Requests for Admissions, since timely responses were made that contained objections, the objections are preserved even without a verification. (See Brown & Weil, 8:1360; 8:1364.) Since the verification was not provided until after the motion was filed, sanctions are warranted in the amount of $1560 as Ruffin did not prevail on all issues.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by email directly to the Judge Pro Tem at rkane1089@aol.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
